title: claude-engine provider → route through CLI seam
notes: Wires apps/claude-engine/lib/provider.js to call Claude via the claude-client seam (no API key) instead of the unwired anthropicProvider / mock echo. Prompt still to author.
prompt:
Wire the LLM provider module to call Claude through the local CLI seam (no
API key, no SDK) instead of the mock echo.

PREREQUISITE CHECK (do this first, STOP if it fails):
Verify that `src/claudeClient.js` exists in the project root. If it is missing,
stop and tell the user to run Delivery #1 (claude-client) first — this
delivery depends on that module.

BACKGROUND:
`lib/provider.js` defines the LLM provider interface used throughout this
server. The interface contract is:

  name:  string
  model: string
  async complete({ system, prompt, model }) → { text, usage, provider, model }

The existing `mockProvider()` echoes input deterministically; it stays in place
as the offline fallback. The `anthropicProvider()` stub throws — ignore it.
`getProvider()` selects the active provider from the environment.

TASK:
Add a `cliProvider()` to `lib/provider.js` that routes completions through
the `ask()` function from `src/claudeClient.js`. Select it automatically when
`ENGINE_LLM=cli`. No API key or network call involved.

1. Import `ask` from `../src/claudeClient.js` (path relative to
   `lib/provider.js`; adjust if your directory layout differs).

2. Implement `cliProvider({ model } = {})` returning an object with:
   - `name: 'cli'`
   - `model: model || 'claude-cli'`   (cosmetic; the CLI picks the real model)
   - `async complete({ system, prompt, model: m } = {})`:
       • Build a single combined prompt string. Recommended format:
           `<system>\n${system}\n</system>\n\n${prompt}`
         If `system` is empty/undefined, omit the `<system>` wrapper and
         pass `prompt` alone — do not send empty XML tags.
       • Call `await ask(combined)` (no extra opts needed).
       • If the result's `status` is not `'ok'`, throw an Error whose message
         is the result's `output` string so callers get a meaningful rejection.
       • On success, return:
           {
             text:     result.output,
             usage:    estimateUsage(combined, result.output),
             provider: 'cli',
             model:    m || 'claude-cli',
           }

3. Add a private helper `estimateUsage(inputStr, outputStr)` that counts
   whitespace-separated tokens and returns `{ input, output }`. The CLI does
   not expose token counts; this is a rough proxy so callers expecting a usage
   object are not broken.

4. Update `getProvider(env = process.env)`:
   - If `env.ENGINE_LLM === 'cli'`, return `cliProvider()`.
   - If `env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY && env.ENGINE_LLM === 'anthropic'`, return
     the existing anthropic stub (or a real implementation if present).
   - Otherwise return `mockProvider()`.

5. Keep `mockProvider()` and `getProvider()` export signatures unchanged so
   existing callers need zero edits.

Add `test/provider.test.js` (node:test) that:
  - skips gracefully if `claude --version` fails (no CLI = no live test).
  - instantiates `cliProvider()` and calls `complete({ prompt: 'Say only: pong' })`.
  - asserts `result.text` contains 'pong' and `result.provider === 'cli'`.
  - asserts that a non-ok result (simulate by passing a prompt designed to
    produce a permission block, OR by temporarily patching `ask` to return
    `{ status: 'error', output: 'test error' }`) causes `complete()` to throw.

Show me the file shape (new export list, cliProvider signature, updated
getProvider branch) BEFORE writing the implementation.
